_meta.note: Availability contract for the rmcp stdio adapter. system_discover remains callable when the daemon is unavailable and reports daemon availability for every tool. Platform truth precedes daemon reachability: a tool that can only return UnsupportedPlatform on this host reports available:false with its platform reason even when the daemon is up. US3a/TC53: the four pty_command_* tools now have a PTY backend on BOTH unix (pty-process) and Windows (ConPTY/portable-pty), so they are platform-available on unix+windows (available:true when the daemon is up). The shell_session_*/workspace_snapshot_* tools remain unix-only (the session runtime is #[cfg(unix)]); Windows session support is a separate slice. This example was captured on a Windows host with the daemon DOWN: pty_command_* report daemon_unavailable (platform is fine), session/snapshot tools report the unix-only platform reason. US6/T056: the payload also carries a READ-ONLY omni_status capability matrix assembled honestly from live runtime + config: shell_exec reports capability presence (the lane is wired) not the allow_shell cap value; sessions mirror the unix-only session runtime; pty mirrors the dual unix/ConPTY backend with platform (posix|windows_conpty|unavailable); privileged_helper is ALWAYS available:false reason threat_review_pending (P4 is plan-only); remote_targets counts targets.toml entries and how many answered a bounded probe. With the daemon DOWN here, the daemon-gated rows (shell_exec/sessions/pty) all report available:false; pty.platform stays honest (windows_conpty). TC22 A3: the catalogue now includes file_write (the policy-gated filesystem-WRITE tool that makes paths.write_allow enforceable), so the live tool count is 50; like the other daemon-backed file tools it reports daemon_unavailable while the daemon is down.

response_example_daemon_unavailable.daemon_error: daemon ipc error [Internal]: daemon unavailable at startup

tools.name: system_discover
tools.description: Return adapter version, MCP spec, policy profile, tool catalogue.

tools.name: health
tools.description: Daemon liveness ping; returns uptime seconds.

tools.name: policy_status
tools.description: Active policy profile and bounded per-call caps.

tools.name: self_check
tools.description: Re-run the daemon self-check; bounded text report.

tools.name: command_start_combed
tools.description: Start a non-PTY argv command; bounded metadata response. No raw stdout/stderr.

tools.name: run_and_watch
tools.description: One-shot: start a command, wait (bounded) for its rule signals + exit, return both. Quiet command returns a receipt, not an error.

tools.name: command_status
tools.description: Lifecycle + counters lookup for a previously started job.

tools.name: command_stop
tools.description: Force-kill a running combed (non-PTY) command by job_id; returns final bounded counters. Never returns raw output.

tools.name: shell_exec
tools.description: Run ONE shell line (pipelines/compounds/redirects) through the comb pipeline; requires allow_shell; combed, never raw.

tools.name: command_output_tail
tools.description: Read the last N lines of a command's captured output WITHOUT a rule. For one-off/exploratory commands whose format you don't know (df -h, docker system df): start it, then read its tail here. Bounded: 200 lines / 64 KiB, truncation-flagged. For recurring signals, define a rule instead.

tools.name: bucket_events_since
tools.description: Cursor read of a bucket. Bounded; severity / kind filters supported.

tools.name: bucket_wait
tools.description: Realtime wait on a bucket. Heartbeat returned on timeout.

tools.name: bucket_summary
tools.description: Bucket counters + severity histogram. No raw stream content.

tools.name: event_context
tools.description: Bounded context window around an event. Pointer-based.

tools.name: registry_search
tools.description: FTS search over persisted rule definitions. Bounded.

tools.name: registry_get
tools.description: Fetch a rule definition by id and optional version.

tools.name: registry_upsert
tools.description: Insert a new immutable (rule_id, version+1) row from a JSON definition.

tools.name: registry_test
tools.description: Dry-run a rule against bounded samples; never persists, no raw stream lane.

tools.name: registry_activate
tools.description: Activate (rule_id, version?) for every newly-started command.

tools.name: registry_import_pack
tools.description: Import a curated rule pack by name; optionally activate it in one call.

tools.name: registry_deactivate
tools.description: Deactivate (rule_id, version); future commands skip the rule.

tools.name: registry_list_active
tools.description: Snapshot of every currently-active rule (id + version + severity).

tools.name: registry_suggest_from_samples
tools.description: Suggest candidate parsing rules from raw output samples via pure heuristics. Returns DRAFT proposals + confidence + the explicit test->upsert->activate next steps. NEVER activates or persists a rule.

tools.name: file_read_window
tools.description: Bounded line/byte window read of one file. Never returns the whole file.

tools.name: file_search
tools.description: Bounded substring search over one file. Returns structured match pointers + capped snippets.

tools.name: file_write
tools.description: Write UTF-8 content to one file. Policy-gated by paths.write_allow; audited before write; bounded size; atomic. Mutating + non-idempotent.

tools.name: file_watch_start
tools.description: Start a daemon-owned file watch bound to a bucket; emits signal only when scoped rules match.

tools.name: file_watch_stop
tools.description: Stop a previously started file watch by watch_id.

tools.name: file_watch_list
tools.description: Snapshot of every currently-live file watch.

tools.name: pty_command_start
tools.description: Start an interactive non-shell argv command attached to a PTY. Bounded metadata only.

tools.name: pty_command_write_stdin
tools.description: Write bounded stdin bytes to a running PTY job. Denied while a secret prompt is active.

tools.name: pty_command_stop
tools.description: Stop a previously started PTY job by job_id. Returns final counters.

tools.name: pty_command_list
tools.description: Snapshot of every currently-live PTY job (including secret_prompt_active).

tools.name: shell_session_start
tools.description: Start a persistent shell session (sticky cwd/env across sends). Requires allow_session; denied by default; combed output only.
tools.unavailable_reason: shell-session runtime unavailable on this platform (unix-only)

tools.name: shell_session_exec
tools.description: Run ONE line in a session shell; returns the combed signals it produced. Sticky cwd/env from the prior lines; never a raw stream.
tools.unavailable_reason: shell-session runtime unavailable on this platform (unix-only)

tools.name: shell_session_status
tools.description: Session lifecycle state, current cwd, and a bounded env snapshot.
tools.unavailable_reason: shell-session runtime unavailable on this platform (unix-only)

tools.name: shell_session_stop
tools.description: Stop a session (graceful then forced); reports the terminal state.
tools.unavailable_reason: shell-session runtime unavailable on this platform (unix-only)

tools.name: shell_session_list
tools.description: Snapshot of every currently-live session (id, state, cwd, last_active).
tools.unavailable_reason: shell-session runtime unavailable on this platform (unix-only)

tools.name: workspace_snapshot_create
tools.description: Save a session's cwd + bounded env as a restorable workspace snapshot.
tools.unavailable_reason: shell-session runtime unavailable on this platform (unix-only)

tools.name: workspace_snapshot_apply
tools.description: Restore a workspace snapshot's cwd/env into a session.
tools.unavailable_reason: shell-session runtime unavailable on this platform (unix-only)

tools.name: runtime_state
tools.description: Bounded aggregate runtime snapshot: probes, buckets, active rule scopes.

tools.name: probe_list
tools.description: Flat list of every live probe across all runtimes (command / pty / file watch).

tools.name: probe_status
tools.description: Bounded per-probe lookup by probe_id. Returns UnknownProbe if not live.

tools.name: subscription_open
tools.description: Open ONE predicate-routed subscription over many sources; returns an opaque sub_id + boot_id.

tools.name: subscription_pull
tools.description: Blocking multiplexed pull of matched events + per-source liveness. Idle returns SUCCESS empty+liveness.

tools.name: subscription_list
tools.description: Bounded snapshot of open subscriptions.

tools.name: subscription_close
tools.description: Close a subscription by sub_id; idempotent.

tools.name: subscription_seek
tools.description: Reposition a subscription's offset for one bucket (explicit re-read); clamped, never an error.

tools.name: target_list
tools.description: List registered remote-federation targets + reachability (from targets.toml; default none = local-only). Read-only; answers from adapter-side state, so it stays available when the local daemon is down.

tools.name: target_probe
tools.description: Probe ONE target's health over its operator-forwarded LOCAL socket; returns reachable + daemon_version. Requires the local daemon (confirms allow_remote) so reports daemon_unavailable while the daemon is down.

availability_invariants.pty_unavailable_reason_when_no_runtime: PTY runtime unavailable on this platform
availability_invariants.session_unavailable_reason_when_no_runti: shell-session runtime unavailable on this platform (unix-only)
